title: Permission denied opening /dev/stderr etc by Apache2
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Hi!

After upgrading to Fedora 31 I was excited to try Podman, but it fails when running an old Apache image I have (it's working on Docker, but it fails with podman with *and* without cgroups2):

```
podman run --rm -it richard87/php-base:v7.3.5 bash
Trying to pull docker.io/richard87/php-base:v7.3.5...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 78873f480bce done
...
Copying blob 6c8510ceb725 done
Copying config 67179a4fea done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

appuser@a86b4832b584:/var/www$ apache2
apache2             apache2-foreground  apache2ctl          
appuser@a86b4832b584:/var/www$ apache2-foreground 
AH00112: Warning: DocumentRoot [/var/www/public] does not exist
AH00558: apache2: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.0.2.100. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
(13)Permission denied: AH00091: apache2: could not open error log file /var/log/apache2/error.log.
AH00015: Unable to open logs

appuser@a86b4832b584:/var/www$ echo "hello" > /dev/stdout 
bash: /dev/stdout: Permission denied

appuser@a86b4832b584:/var/www$ ls -la /var/log/apache2/
total 8
drwxrwxrwx 2 www-data www-data 4096 Mar 27  2019 .
drwxr-xr-x 2 root     root     4096 Mar 27  2019 ..
lrwxrwxrwx 1 www-data www-data   11 Mar 27  2019 access.log -> /dev/stdout
lrwxrwxrwx 1 www-data www-data   11 Mar 27  2019 error.log -> /dev/stderr
lrwxrwxrwx 1 www-data www-data   11 Mar 27  2019 other_vhosts_access.log -> /dev/stdout
appuser@a86b4832b584:/var/www$ 
``` 

**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman info --debug

  Distribution:

  IDMappings:

    - container_id: 0

    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 423706624
  MemTotal: 42019442688
  OCIRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.10.2-1.fc31.x86_64

    version: |-
      crun version 0.10.2
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 20714614784
  SwapTotal: 21093150720
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald

      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  uptime: 145h 55m 54.5s (Approximately 6.04 days)
registries:
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
rpm -q podman
podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running on physical Fedora 31, tested with and without the Cgroup fix:
`sudo grubby --update-kernel=ALL --args="systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=0"`

comments.body: EPERM smells like it could be SELinux. Could you run the container with `--security-opt label=disable` and see if that fixes it?

I have selinux disabled on the host with cgroups1, 
and selinux enabled on the host with cgroup2, I tried `--security-opt label=disable`on the host with cgroup2, but the same error... 
(I have no idea if cgroup version have anything to do with it)

cgroup1 host (workstation):
``` 
$ sestatus
SELinux status:                 disabled

$ podman run --rm -it --security-opt label=disable richard87/php-base:v7.3.5 bash
appuser@26c09055a33f:/var/www$ apache2-foreground 
AH00112: Warning: DocumentRoot [/var/www/public] does not exist
AH00558: apache2: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.0.2.100. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
(13)Permission denied: AH00091: apache2: could not open error log file /var/log/apache2/error.log.
AH00015: Unable to open logs
appuser@26c09055a33f:/var/www$ sestatus
bash: sestatus: command not found
appuser@26c09055a33f:/var/www$ exit
```

cgroup2 host (laptop):
```
$ sestatus
SELinux status:                 enabled
SELinuxfs mount:                /sys/fs/selinux
SELinux root directory:         /etc/selinux
Loaded policy name:             targeted
Current mode:                   enforcing
Mode from config file:          enforcing
Policy MLS status:              enabled
Policy deny_unknown status:     allowed
Memory protection checking:     actual (secure)
Max kernel policy version:      31

$ podman run --rm -it --security-opt label=disable 67179a4feaf0cb7c4e365db0eec67a234a12d0d8cbe7f89a19ddf37bbbe72bd4 bash
appuser@86c1a7bbe493:/var/www$ apache2-foreground 
AH00112: Warning: DocumentRoot [/var/www/public] does not exist
AH00558: apache2: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.0.2.100. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
(13)Permission denied: AH00091: apache2: could not open error log file /var/log/apache2/error.log.
AH00015: Unable to open logs
```
can you try to add `--group-add tty` to run?

Yup, that worked nicely :) 

Thanks for your support!
opened a PR in crun to address the issue: https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/175

I'm trying to use the official PHP Apache image at Docker Hub, but they do this:
https://github.com/docker-library/php/blob/42c9bfecb0f7f59efebd10bd7345dbedfcdcdb6e/7.4-rc/buster/apache/Dockerfile#L85-L88:
```
# logs should go to stdout / stderr
	ln -sfT /dev/stderr "$APACHE_LOG_DIR/error.log"; \
	ln -sfT /dev/stdout "$APACHE_LOG_DIR/access.log"; \
	ln -sfT /dev/stdout "$APACHE_LOG_DIR/other_vhosts_access.log"; \
	chown -R --no-dereference "$APACHE_RUN_USER:$APACHE_RUN_GROUP" "$APACHE_LOG_DIR"
```

And that fails when USER is not root and the container is running without `-ti`...
```
podman run --user=1000 --rm php:7.4.0RC6-apache-buster apache2-foreground --help
AH00558: apache2: Could not reliably determine the server's fully qualified domain name, using 10.0.2.100. Set the 'ServerName' directive globally to suppress this message
(13)Permission denied: AH00072: make_sock: could not bind to address [::]:80
(13)Permission denied: AH00072: make_sock: could not bind to address 0.0.0.0:80
no listening sockets available, shutting down
AH00015: Unable to open logs
```

*Should it work?*

@Richard87 what runtime are you using?

If you are using crun, could you verify it is the latest version?

It seems I'm using `crun-0.10.6-1` (which is the latest release on github):

```
podman info
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.3
  CgroupVersion: v2
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.2-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.2, commit: 186a550ba0866ce799d74006dab97969a2107979'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 3661582336
  MemTotal: 42019450880
  OCIRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.10.6-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.10.6
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

```

It seems to work, I think there actually is a bug in my docker image that got visible after upgrading to podman...

```
podman run --rm --user 1000 php:apache echo "test" > /dev/stdout && echo "test 2" > /dev/stderr
test
test 2
```
